tasks.description: Prioritization matrix UI [2/2] — effort/impact score selectors on step + touchpoint detail panels, new /prioritization page with quadrant chart (CSS-positioned divs), tab/section filter dropdowns, sidebar nav, workspace-shell reserved path
acceptance_criteria: Step detail panel shows effort (1-5) and impact (1-5) score selectors below maturity section, reusing MATURITY_OPTIONS button pattern (click to set, click again to clear)
Touchpoint detail panel shows same effort/impact score selectors (reuse same pattern — touchpoint has pain_score as reference)
Score changes persist via existing PATCH routes (effort_score, impact_score fields already in EDITABLE_FIELDS)
New page at /w/[workspaceId]/prioritization with server component (page.tsx) fetching steps+touchpoints+sections+stages+tabs
Client view component (prioritization-view.tsx) renders quadrant chart using CSS absolute positioning within relative container — NO charting library

X-axis = effort (1-5), Y-axis = impact (1-5). Quadrant labels: Quick Wins (low effort, high impact), Major Projects (high effort, high impact), Fill-Ins (low effort, low impact), Deprioritize (high effort, low impact)
Items plotted as dots colored by parent section/stage using deterministic name hash → HSL palette (8 colors). Radix Tooltip on hover shows item name
Click dot navigates to correct canvas tab
Two filter dropdowns above chart: Tab (select, default All) and Section/Stage (select, filtered by selected tab, default All)
Sidebar nav link added for Prioritization (between Perspectives and Runbooks)
workspace-shell.tsx reserved paths includes 'prioritization'

similar_files: src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/perspectives/compare/page.tsx — similar page structure (server fetch + client view)
patterns_to_follow: Maturity score selector UI in step-detail-panel.tsx lines 360-382 — clickable buttons with MATURITY_OPTIONS array, click toggles value via handleFieldUpdate, selected shows colored background
Perspective comparison page pattern — server page fetches workspace data, passes to client view component
Sidebar nav items defined around line 41-46 — array of {label, icon, href} objects
workspace-shell.tsx line 47 — exclusion array for reserved path segments
Radix Tooltip already in deps — use for dot hover labels
Deterministic color: hash entity name string → pick from 8-color HSL palette. Simple string hash (sum char codes) mod 8
learnings_relevant: workspace-shell route exclusion list — MUST add 'prioritization' to prevent canvas view rendering
apiFetch unwraps envelope — client functions return apiFetch<T>() directly

tasks.special_instructions: Data layer already done in iter 105 (migration 021, types updated, PATCH routes updated). This task is UI-only. Effort/impact selectors: define EFFORT_OPTIONS and IMPACT_OPTIONS arrays (1-5) with labels like 'Very Low'/'Low'/'Medium'/'High'/'Very High'. Place below maturity section in step panel. For touchpoint panel, add in similar location to pain_score section. Quadrant chart: use CSS grid or absolute positioning — items are positioned as percentage of container (effort_score/5 * 100% for x, inverted impact_score for y so high impact is top). Filter by tab first, then section/stage within that tab. Empty state when no items have scores.

tasks.description: Remove redundant step count display on runbook cards — metadata row shows count AND progress bar shows count
acceptance_criteria: Only ONE step count display per runbook card (keep progress bar label, remove metadata row count)
Progress bar still shows '{resolved} / {total}' text (IMP-046)
Metadata row no longer shows '{resolved}/{total} steps' text (IMP-014 removal)
Visual balance maintained — no awkward gaps from removal

patterns_to_follow: IMP-046 added progress bar text at line ~124
IMP-014 added metadata row count at line ~114

tasks.special_instructions: Simple removal. Find the metadata row that shows '{resolved}/{total} steps' and remove it. Keep the progress bar label ('{resolved} / {total}'). Verify the card layout still looks balanced after removal.

tasks.description: Replace native confirm() with Radix Dialog for Delete Workspace action — match clone dialog pattern (IMP-028)
acceptance_criteria: Delete Workspace uses Radix Dialog instead of native confirm()
Dialog includes workspace name: 'Permanently delete "{workspace.name}"? This cannot be undone.'
Cancel button (secondary variant) and Delete button (destructive variant/red)
Follows same pattern as clone dialog (IMP-028) — confirmDeleteOpen useState boolean
Zero window.confirm() calls remain in settings page

similar_files: Same file — clone dialog pattern from IMP-028 (confirmCloneOpen state + Radix Dialog)
patterns_to_follow: IMP-028 clone dialog pattern in settings/page.tsx — Dialog/DialogContent/DialogHeader/DialogTitle/DialogDescription/DialogFooter with useState boolean
IMP-012 runbook Complete/Cancel dialogs — same Radix Dialog pattern with destructive variant
learnings_relevant: BUG-023/024 — use DialogPrimitive.Title from @radix-ui/react-dialog if needed for a11y, though ui/dialog.tsx DialogTitle should work for new dialogs following existing pattern

tasks.special_instructions: Mirror the existing clone dialog pattern exactly. Add confirmDeleteOpen state. Replace the confirm() call in handleDelete with setConfirmDeleteOpen(true). Move actual delete logic into the dialog's confirm button onClick. Use destructive/red styling for the Delete button. Include workspace.name in dialog description.

shared_files.note: database.ts and client.ts already have effort_score/impact_score types and API wrappers from iter 105. No modifications needed.

testing_plan.regression_reason: Regression ran last iteration (105) at 12/12 PASS. Risk score 2 for this iteration (UI-only changes, no data model). Next regression trigger: iteration 110 (cadence floor) or risk score ≥3.
